description: Equipment-type-specific IQ checklists for qualification-relevant verification. Per ISO 17665, 21 CFR 820.70, FDA IQ guidance. ItemIDs are illustrative; engine may map to ruleset IDs.

items.description: Equipment received per specification; no damage
items.expected: Equipment matches PO/specification; no shipping damage; serial number recorded

items.description: Equipment installed per supplier DQ; correct orientation
items.expected: Installation per manufacturer instructions; leveling and seismic anchoring per spec; chamber orientation correct
Steam pressure, purity, dryness per supplier spec; condensate traps verified; pipe sizing and insulation documented
Vacuum pump capacity and leak rate per spec; water-ring pump cooling (if applicable)
Voltage, amperage, phase per spec; grounding verified; UPS/backup if critical cycles
Pressure and quality per spec; dryer/filter verified where used
Flow, temperature, quality per supplier spec
Drain capacity, temperature tolerance, routing per spec
Sensor specs per drawings; placement verified; NIST-traceable calibration certs available
Pressure sensor specs per drawings; calibration certs available
Vacuum gauge specs and calibration per supplier
Door cannot open during cycle; interlock bypass procedure documented
Pressure relief valve present; over-temperature and over-pressure alarms functional
Emergency stop functional per manufacturer
HMI screens per spec; cycle parameters visible; alarm display functional
All cycle programs available per spec; program selection verified
Data logging capability; export to file and/or printout functional; audit trail enabled if Part 11

items.description: Audit trail (if HMI used for release)
items.expected: Audit trail enabled; tamper-evident per 21 CFR Part 11

items.description: Backup and restore (if HMI used for release)
items.expected: Backup/restore procedure documented; tested per CSV
Supplier manuals, P&IDs, electrical drawings; calibration certs for critical sensors
SOPs drafted for operation, loading, Bowie-Dick, BI; calibration schedule established
Critical spare parts identified; list documented
Adequate clearance, ventilation; utilities connections labeled and traceable
NIST-traceable calibration for TCs, pressure gauges used in qualification; within cal
Operators trained on SOPs; training records on file
Equipment ID, serial, location traceable to asset registry
FAT/SAT reports reviewed; deviations closed; supplier escalation path documented
